summary: SYNAPTIQ P1 Institutional Layer end-to-end testing PASSED. Backend: 4/4 pytest test_institutions_phase.py PASS + 6/6 new smoke tests (test_institutions_smoke_iter13.py) PASS — directory >=2, my_membership populated, analytics, governance CRUD+audit, seat patch, email-domain claim auto-approve. Frontend: every review-request scenario verified — sidebar nav-institutions, directory (search/type/country filters + 2 default cards), create-institution flow ('Institution registered' toast + Pytest University card appeared), detail tabs (all 8 testids), 6 overview KPIs, research-health with 4 component bars (Publications/Grants/Funding $/Reputation), Researchers tab (1 approved member for creator), Units tab full lifecycle (create modal → unit-card → /units/{id} with name+breadcrumb+4 KPIs), Unit member management (pick-member → save → 'Members updated' toast → list updates), Govern tab (seats 1/5 with 'Seat capacity updated' toast, members rows, audit log with unit_members_add/unit_created/institution_created), Claim flow as elena (button visible, 'Request submitted — pending admin approval' toast), Analytics tabs (Publications/Funding/Reputation all render with testids), responsive at 1366×768 (body horizontal overflow = 0, tabs row fits in viewport). Cleanup: Pytest University deleted via DELETE API; directory back to original 2.

minor.issue: Migrated institutions ETH Zurich and SYNAPTIQ HQ have owner_id=null, admin_ids=[], and seats.total=0. Ownership is encoded only in institution_memberships.role='owner' (admin@synaptiq.io is the owner-role member of SYNAPTIQ HQ). Functionally correct (govern tab respects role), but the denormalised owner_id/admin_ids fields on the institution doc are not populated by the migration script — consider backfilling for consistency with newly created institutions which DO populate these.

Review request says 'Elena is approved member of ETH Zurich', but the migration linked Elena into SYNAPTIQ HQ (role=researcher, seat=personal). ETH Zurich does have 3 migrated members (other demo users). Not a bug — just a doc/reality drift to flag.

issues: After claim is submitted with status=pending, the page still shows '1 researchers' (creator) but does not surface a 'Your request is pending' badge on the header — toast disappears after a few seconds, then there is no persistent visual cue that user already claimed. Minor UX polish.
seats-total-input is an unbounded number field. Entering 0 or negative values is not blocked client-side. Backend likely validates, but a min='0' / step='1' on the input would be a nice guard.

action_items: (Optional) Backfill owner_id and admin_ids on migrated institution docs to match the convention used by newly created institutions.
(Optional polish) Add a persistent 'Claim pending' badge on the institution detail header for users whose membership.status='pending', so the state is visible after the toast fades.
(Optional polish) Add min='0' / step='1' to seats-total-input.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/institutions/{iid} only adds my_membership when a record exists — consider always emitting my_membership: null so the FE doesn't have to do `if ('my_membership' in body)` introspection. Same applies to /api/units/{uid}.

Audit log endpoint returns plain list; consider pagination (cursor or offset/limit) — current SYNAPTIQ HQ list already has many entries and will grow unbounded. Same for /api/institutions/{iid}/members.

routers/institutions.py is starting to grow (571 lines, 14+ routes). Consider splitting into routers/institutions/{directory.py, units.py, members.py, analytics.py, audit.py} for maintainability (file size guideline >700 lines mentioned in our review checklist — currently within budget but trending).

Migration script leaves owner_id=null on migrated institutions despite assigning a membership with role='owner'. This denormalisation drift could confuse downstream consumers that check institution.owner_id directly rather than membership.role.

Console produced 4×401 errors on the elena claim flow (background heartbeat/notifications calls during the brief unauth window after sign-out). Not user-visible; consider suppressing or short-circuiting these requests when no auth cookie is present.
seed_data_creation: Created and CLEANED UP: 'Pytest University' institution (id 6a2dd5f9d4c0b643ac656fc9), 'Pytest Lab' unit under it (id 6a2dd626d4c0b643ac656fcc), 1 elena claim membership (deleted with the institution). Backend pytest tests also create+delete 'Pytest Lab' and 'Pytest Domain Inst' / 'Pytest Seats Inst' each run. Final state confirmed: GET /api/institutions returns total=2 (ETH Zurich + SYNAPTIQ HQ) — back to baseline.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 13 Institutional Layer is green. Pytest regression suites: `BASE_URL=https://idea-to-pub.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_institutions_phase.py /app/backend/tests/test_institutions_smoke_iter13.py -v` → 10/10. Seeded data (ETH Zurich, SYNAPTIQ HQ) is migration-only and idempotent. Govern tab in UI gates on user.role==admin OR membership.role in ('owner','admin') — platform admin sees it everywhere. Claim flow auto-approves when user.email domain is in institution.email_domains; otherwise status=pending and admin must POST /api/institutions/{iid}/members/{uid}/decide. Audit endpoint records action strings: institution_created, unit_created, unit_members_add, role_assigned, seat_assigned, member_approved, member_denied, etc. Reputation cache is 24h; no UI force-refresh.
rca of the issue: No blocking issues. Two minor migration data inconsistencies flagged (owner_id null on migrated institutions; review-request context drift about elena's assignment). Three optional UX polish items listed. Backend pytest 4/4 phase + 6/6 smoke = 10/10. Frontend every flow specified in the 17-item review request was reproduced and passed.
